summary: Iteration 43 - MANUTRIX Phase 1 Block A (master role, Plantas CRUD, Master cleanup, redesigned Kanban, enhanced OS filters). Backend pytest: 20/20 PASS after fix. Frontend regression PASS. Found and fixed one HIGH-severity bug in PUT /api/plantas/{id} (audit_field_changes called with wrong signature -> Motor DB object encoded into BSON -> 500).

issues: Pre-existing Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warnings during initial render (documented iteration 41-42, cosmetic only).

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED by tester - server.py:1671: update_planta called audit_field_changes(db, 'planta', planta_id, existing, {**existing, **updates}, user) but the helper signature is audit_field_changes(entity_type, entity_id, entity_label, old_doc, new_data, user, motivo=''). Passing the Motor `db` object caused 'bson.errors.InvalidDocument: cannot encode object: AsyncIOMotorDatabase' -> 500 on every PUT /api/plantas/{id}. Fixed to: audit_field_changes('planta', planta_id, f'Planta {existing.get("nome","")}', existing, updates, user).
server.py:1689 - master_cleanup uses `targets: List[str] = Query(default=[])`. Frontend posts targets as query string (works), but POST body would not be parsed. Document this for API consumers.

server.py:1697 - org_filter falls back to {} when user has no organization_id, which means cleanup could delete cross-org docs for a master user without an org_id. Consider rejecting cleanup if org_id is empty rather than silently global-deleting.
deps.py:107-111 - check_admin_only allows both 'admin' and 'master' (intentional). Naming is misleading; consider renaming to check_admin_or_master for clarity.
App.js:1977 - 'Limpeza' string appears as a chart legend label for OS tipo 'limpeza_organizacao' on Dashboard, which can confuse role-visibility text-based tests; not a bug but worth noting.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all critical flows verified visually + via selectors)

test_credentials: master@manutrix.com/master123 (verified, role=master), admin@manutrix.com/admin123 (verified, role=admin), tecnico@manutrix.com/tecnico123 (verified, role=tecnico). Master user already seeded in MongoDB.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_Planta* via API and one TEST_UI_Planta via UI; cleaned up via soft-delete tests. Cleanup endpoint exercised against 'notificacoes' target.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Block A complete. New routes: /api/plantas (CRUD, admin+master can write), /api/master/cleanup, /api/master/prepare-production, /api/master/admin-actions (master only). OS list page route is /os (NOT /ordens-servico). Click 'Filtros' button to reveal Tipo/Área/Responsável/Disciplina selectors. Master user seeded with role 'master' in users collection.
rca of the issue: Bug RCA — PUT /api/plantas/{id} returned 500. Root cause: server.py line 1671 invoked `audit_field_changes(db, 'planta', planta_id, existing, {**existing, **updates}, user)` but the helper's actual signature is `audit_field_changes(entity_type, entity_id, entity_label, old_doc, new_data, user, motivo='')`. The Motor `db` instance was placed into `entity_type` and downstream Motor was asked to encode it into a BSON document, raising `bson.errors.InvalidDocument: cannot encode object: AsyncIOMotorDatabase`. Reproduction: log in as admin/master, create a planta, PUT /api/plantas/{id} with any payload. Mitigation applied: changed call to `audit_field_changes('planta', planta_id, f'Planta {existing.get("nome","")}', existing, updates, user)`. Test re-run confirms 200 and persistence.
